listeners.purpose: On boot, populate the in-memory rbac:permissions:catalogue:{application_id} + rbac:role_definitions:{application_id} caches. Reduces cold-request latency by ~50ms on the first gated route hit.
Kicks off SeedTenantDefaultRolesJob when a new tenant is created. Runs async because seeding can take ~500ms for a full role set.
GDPR Art. 17 compliance. Cascade-delete via FK, but this listener writes the compliance-audit entry + emits count metric + broadcasts cache invalidation for every principal in the tenant.

When a new User is provisioned on an Application, auto-assign the tenant's default role (usually `member` OR the tenant-configured default). Skipped when the provisioning payload specifies an explicit role list.
Purge the suspended user's rbac:permissions:User:{user_id}:* cache keys. Auth's HasSessionRevocation trait also revokes the user's PATs — the two chains run in parallel.
Hard-cascade: enumerate every model_has_roles row where model_id = user.id + fire RoleRevokedFromPrincipal per row + delete pivot rows. Auth handles session revocation via its own chain.
For every User row referencing the erased Identity, revoke every active role assignment. Cross-Application: a single Identity may have User rows on N Applications; every one gets the full revocation.

The auth chain revokes every session, so on the next login the resolver will cold-resolve. This listener PRE-WARMS the cache for the user + their default Application so the FIRST post-password-change login is fast.
A new grant landed — purge the affected principal's permission cache so the resolver sees the grant on the next check.
Same as above, for the revoke path.
When Alice delegates all her roles to Bob, Bob's permission cache must be invalidated so his next check picks up the delegator's permission set.

When a tenant downgrades from Medium to Small (loses `custom_roles`), do NOT auto-delete their custom roles. Mark them `is_active=false` so they remain in reports + can be re-activated on re-upgrade. Existing assignments continue to resolve for the graceful-degradation window.
